title: podman build --layers: runtime panic
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:



# **kind bug**



**Description**

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. https://github.com/vbatts/copr-build-bazel

2. continually call `podman build --layers .`

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

```shell
[vbatts@getdown] {master *+} ~/src/github.com/vbatts/copr-build-bazel$ podman build --layers .
STEP 1: FROM fedora
STEP 2: WORKDIR /build
--> Using cache a69a57407f64bdff1da2b615dd1bed3c6172944e2d431a9559483959cb6c3350
STEP 3: FROM a69a57407f64bdff1da2b615dd1bed3c6172944e2d431a9559483959cb6c3350
STEP 4: RUN dnf install -y 'dnf-command(builddep)' rpm-build java-1.8.0-openjdk-devel zlib-devel pkgconfig python gcc-c++ make
--> Using cache 4a7d7c39508704e583b59ee5da6bdc700d5ac2c0eb4f71b2cee7e470332ccdb8
STEP 5: FROM 4a7d7c39508704e583b59ee5da6bdc700d5ac2c0eb4f71b2cee7e470332ccdb8
STEP 6: ADD . /build
error checking if cached image exists from a previous build: error checking if copied files match: error resolving symlinks and comparing modified times: "/home/vbatts/src/github.com/vbatts/copr-build-bazel": panic: runtime error: slice bounds out of range

goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/imagebuildah.modTimeIsGreater(0x7ffc3fa1f164, 0x33, 0x7ffc3fa1f198, 0x33, 0x7ffc3fa1f1cc, 0x1e, 0xc4202bb698, 0x55f555, 0x4158c4)
        /builddir/build/BUILD/libpod-66d34998316adac9f9d3c0f422705bf4b02011d5/_build/src/github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/imagebuildah/chroot_symlink.go:142 +0x3a2
github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/imagebuildah.resolveSymlinkTimeModified()
        /builddir/build/BUILD/libpod-66d34998316adac9f9d3c0f422705bf4b02011d5/_build/src/github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/imagebuildah/chroot_symlink.go:98 +0x211
github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/pkg/reexec.Init(0x1133d00)
        /builddir/build/BUILD/libpod-66d34998316adac9f9d3c0f422705bf4b02011d5/_build/src/github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/pkg/reexec/reexec.go:26 +0x77
main.main()
        /builddir/build/BUILD/libpod-66d34998316adac9f9d3c0f422705bf4b02011d5/_build/src/github.com/containers/libpod/cmd/podman/main.go:51 +0x71
: exit status 2
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

normal build process

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.12.1.1
Go Version:    go1.10.5
Git Commit:    "8a97c0f33b6bd8eabb04f0b8368b78022b53942f-dirty"
Built:         Fri Dec  7 15:01:00 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

  SwapFree: 0

  rootless: true
  uptime: 2144h 16m 7.03s (Approximately 89.33 days)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
@ypu or @edsantiago mind adding a ginkgo system-test to verify this (and confirm it's fixed, when it is)?

Kinda important that build and cache work properly :confused:
@vbatts Can you add the full Dockerfile that you used or a ptr to it?  I looked, may have missed it.

Hi @cevich 
Will add this to system test later after it fixed. Or we can add it now as system test will not always triggered in pr?

Hi @TomSweeneyRedHat 
Try to reproduce this with simpler Dockerfile, and find this may caused by "ADD . xxx"

With following Dockerfile this problem still can reproduced:
```
# cat Dockerfile
FROM fedora
ADD . /build 
```
Test "ADD" with a file or a dir name it will not trigger this. Only "." or "../"will trigger this.

And also find another issue have the same error message like this one:
https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1973
It also panic at a step with  "ADD . xxx":
"STEP 14: ADD . /go/src/github.com/containers/libpod"

So I guess they should be the same.

@ypu system-test is always optional in PR.  Only triggered when magic string in the description.  Do this temporarily when PR is ``[WIP]``, to check if system-test PASS.  Then edit description and remove when ready.  After merge, it will not run again until actual system-test time, during package build process (@edsantiago working on that part more, after new year).
This seems to be fixed now with the last updates to buildah (already vendored).
